name: Freedom Metals Acquisition Corp.
business: Freedom Metals Acquisition Corp. is a special purpose acquisition company formed to effect a merger with one or more businesses, focusing on companies in the critical minerals and mining sector. It has not selected any business combination target and has not initiated substantive discussions. The company seeks to acquire targets with enterprise value over $1 billion.

exposures.sentence: Our primary focus is on companies that own, operate, or are developing assets within the critical minerals and mining sector.

In December 2024, NATO formally identified 12 defense-critical raw materials as essential to the manufacture of advanced military systems ranging from fighter aircraft to precision-guided munitions, radar, and submarines.
According to The Copper Development Association Inc., electric vehicles contain approximately 183 pounds of copper per vehicle, more than three times that of internal combustion vehicles.

Lithium demand rose nearly 30% in 2024, while key battery metals including nickel, cobalt, graphite, and rare earth elements grew by 6 to 8%, driven by EVs, grid expansion, and renewable energy deployment.

Lithium demand rose nearly 30% in 2024, while key battery metals including nickel, cobalt, graphite, and rare earth elements grew by 6 to 8%, driven by EVs, grid expansion, and renewable energy deployment.

At the foundation of this market are structural industrial metals, including aluminum, copper, nickel, zinc, lead, and tin, which form the backbone of global industrial activity and long-duration infrastructure development.

tamClaims.claim: Global critical minerals market estimated at $409.74 billion in 2025 and projected to reach $669.76 billion by 2032, CAGR 6.1% from 2026 to 2032.
tamClaims.sentence: According to a Data Intelligence, the global critical minerals market is large and growing rapidly, estimated at $409.74 billion in 2025 and projected to reach $669.76 billion by 2032, expanding at a CAGR of 6.1% from 2026 to 2032.
This growth is underpinned by a robust M&A environment, with $102.2 billion in mining transactions recorded in 2024 alone according to Mining Technology.
